layman_headline: Crypto market is bleeding — best to stay on the sidelines and wait for a cleaner setup.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in fear mode with Bitcoin dropping below $63K and most altcoins falling hard. While a few coins like Decred have deeply oversold RSI readings that hint at a potential bounce, the broader conditions are hostile for altcoins — Bitcoin dominance is high at 56%, meaning money is flowing away from smaller coins. Geopolitical tension around the Hormuz Strait is adding risk-off pressure across all markets. None of the current candidates offer a compelling risk/reward setup with clear technical confirmation.
layman_action: Stay in cash for now. The market is fearful and falling — trying to catch a knife here has poor odds. Wait for fear to peak and stabilize, or for a strong volume-confirmed reversal in a quality name before committing capital.
expert_reasoning: Scanning all 8 candidates reveals no high-conviction setup. DEXE and LIT are overbought (RSI 77 and 72) after massive 40-70% runs — classic correction territory, not entry zones. GRASS has a sharp -27% dip but shows a bearish engulfing pattern and terrible R/R (0.48x). DCR's RSI of 19.6 is deeply oversold (historically reliable signal per our credibility data with 75% win rate), but the chart bias is -0.7 bearish, price is below all MAs, and the 22% 7-day pump suggests the easy bounce money has already been captured. ETHFI has the best chart bias (+0.6) with a bullish marubozu, but R/R to nearest resistance is only 0.11x — terrible. KITE and XPL lack sufficient technical data. With BTC threatening $62K on geopolitical risks and high BTC dominance crushing alts, the prudent move is to remain in cash and wait for confirmation.
risk_factors: Geopolitical escalation around Hormuz Strait could trigger further risk-off selling across all crypto
BTC dominance at 56% means altcoins face structural headwinds — capital is consolidating into Bitcoin
Fear & Greed at 28 is improving from 24.6 weekly average, but hasn't bottomed convincingly — could drop further
watch_list: BTC price action around $62K support — a breakdown below $61.5K would likely trigger another alt selloff
DCR for a pullback to $11-11.50 with RSI staying oversold and volume confirmation of a reversal
Fear & Greed index — watch for a spike below 20 or a sharp reversal above 35 to signal capitulation or recovery
market_context: The crypto market is in a fear-driven selloff with BTC dropping 2.5% amid geopolitical tensions over the Hormuz Strait. Bitcoin dominance at 56% is squeezing altcoins, and most top-20 coins are red across both 24h and 7d timeframes.
context_banner: Market fear and geopolitical risk dominate — no high-conviction altcoin setups exist right now; patience is the best trade.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) with improving trend from 24.6 weekly avg — not yet at capitulation levels that historically produce the best bounce entries.
Bitcoin threatening $62K as Trump says US will 'run' Hormuz Strait — geopolitical catalyst adding significant downside risk across crypto markets.
DCR RSI at 19.6 is deeply oversold which historically signals bounces (75% win rate in our data), but the 22% weekly rally suggests the bounce may have already played out.
Bitcoin panic-selling may be ending as sellers' profit margins disappear — suggests we're approaching a bottom but haven't confirmed one yet.
ETH derivatives reset signals potential for fresh positioning — worth watching for an ETH-ecosystem entry if broader conditions stabilize.
